items.title: 'Happening in every university': Students sent to Putin's 'meat grinder'

children.content: Lessons look a little different at some universities and technical colleges around Russia this year, as the Kremlin tries to entice students to enlist in the military to cover massive casualties in Ukraine.

A man in a suit looking on.
Cars drive near a billboard which has a picture of solders on it.

Cars drive near a billboard which has a picture of solders on it.

items.title: Mourners farewell gangster a day after gunman targets wrong funeral venue

children.content: Dozens of mourners have turned out to farewell Sydney crime boss Lorenzo Lemalu at a funeral held at Lakemba Mosque, in Sydney's western suburbs.

image.altText: A crowd of people dressed in black clothing surrounding a black and white funeral arbour and black hearse next to a lawn.

items.title: Missing Melbourne teacher allegedly murdered by brother in India

children.content: Indian authorities allege Melbourne maths teacher Sunil Sharma was murdered by his brother and dumped in a canal. He had been in India to prepare a property for sale.

image.altText: Relatives of missing Melbourne man Sunil Sharma who are accused in relation to his disappearance and death

items.title: 'Beyond our capacity' to defend sea trade lanes, Shoebridge says

children.content: Greens defence spokesman David Shoebridge says it is "ridiculous" to think Australia's military could defend the nation's global trade lanes, and acquiring nuclear-powered submarines under AUKUS only invites Australia to a conflict between the United States and China.

image.altText: David Shoebridge wears blue suit jacket, pink shirt and no tie. Speaking in a corridor to the media.

items.title: Trump battles historians for ownership of US presidential records

children.content: The Trump administration is pushing for a return to pre-Nixon days when US presidents could do whatever they wanted with presidential records.

image.altText: Donald Trump holds up a pile of papers titled 'Accomplishments'.

images.altText: Elena Kats-Chernin smiles wearing a black and white flowery dress, blue shawl and elaborate blue necklace.
A planet in a total solar eclipse with text reading ABC Classic 100: Greatest of All Time.

children.content: Tens of thousands of Australians have cast their votes in the 2025 Classic 100: Greatest Of All Time. Join us as we count down to number one.

items.title: Australian composer cracks ABC Classic 100 top 35, but who will be #1?
Luai takes on former halves-partner Cleary in NRL blockbuster

children.content: NRL premiership winning halves Nathan Cleary and Jarome Luai stand on opposite sides as Wests Tigers host the Penrith Panthers in Western Sydney. Follow live.

image.altText: Nathan Cleary and Jarome Luai look at each other

items.title: 'Almost dying wasn't on my bingo card': Biles reveals 'scariest' health scare

children.content: Decorated gymnast Simone Biles shares an undisclosed health scare, telling her social media followers that "almost dying wasn't on my bingo card earlier this week".

image.altText: Female gymnast Simone Biles is seen reaching for the white bar while in competition.

items.title: Inside a Trump-linked plan to turn a communist-era miliary base into a luxe resort

children.content: Ivanka Trump has floated "manifestations" of luxury holidays. Meanwhile, Albanians are claiming "irreversible" ecological damage. Why protests are shaking Albania.

gallery.altText: A excavator is seen among sand.
Concept art of rooms jutting out of a hill.

thumbnail.altText: A excavator is seen among sand.
Concept art of rooms jutting out of a hill.

items.title: As vaccination rates fall in Australia, a British mother has a warning

children.content: Measles killed Rebecca Archer's daughter Renae slowly and silently over a decade. She has a message for the growing number of parents hesitant to have their children vaccinated.

image.altText: A woman with a serious expression holds a picture of a young girl who is smiling.

items.title: Psychologist sounds alarm over looksmaxxing and 'out-mogging'

children.content: While it's a bit of fun for some, the social media trend known as looksmaxxing can have major impacts on young men's physical and mental health.

image.altText: A young man's face. He has dark hair. He looks straight ahead. There is a "web" of triangles in light green across his face.

items.title: How Tasmania's planned new stadium could trigger deal for Hobart Hurricanes

children.content: Cricket's smallest state says it could become a major player in privatisation, with a potential Hobart Hurricanes deal tied to Tasmania's planned Macquarie Point roofed stadium.

gallery.altText: A group of Hobart Hurricanes cricketers stand on a podium and celebrates while holding a trophy as confetti falls around them.
An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

thumbnail.altText: A group of Hobart Hurricanes cricketers stand on a podium and celebrates while holding a trophy as confetti falls around them.
An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

items.title: Police investigate machete brawl at Melbourne's Flinders Street Station

children.content: Police say up to 10 people were fighting inside the station on Saturday night before some boarded a train still armed with machetes.

image.altText: A number of police officers stand on a platform beside a train with a person sitting on the ground near an officer.

items.title: Australia turns its attention to FIFA World Cup after practice games

children.content: The Socceroos have finished their warm-up games, and now turn their attention to Türkiye in their first World Cup match. So, what have we learned from their friendly games against Mexico and Switzerland?

image.altText: Cristian Volpato of Australia competes for the ball against Remo Freuler of Switzerland.

items.title: I loved my body, even when it was expected I wouldn't get to love another

children.content: I'd take a cure for the pain, but I wouldn't take a cure for my appearance. This is who I am and why should I change myself to make other people more comfortable?

gallery.altText: Carly Findlay wears and pink floral dress and stands next to a tree covered in pink flower. Her head is bald. s.

thumbnail.altText: Carly Findlay wears and pink floral dress and stands next to a tree covered in pink flower. Her head is bald. s.

items.title: Meet the man born, raised and married in his local town hall

children.content: Brian Baker, 95, spent the first 21 years of his life in living quarters at the York Town Hall where his father was caretaker.

image.altText: Man on mobility scooter dwarfed by large ornate stage and red velvet curtains

items.title: Planning, packing and nerves: Six Aussies kick off Race Around The World

children.content: Six Australian filmmakers are about to embark on the journey of a lifetime on Race Around The World. Here's how they're preparing for the unknown.

image.altText: Six people huddle together and smile

items.title: Debunking Australian weather's made-up terms and pseudoscience

children.content: Meteorological rhetoric in Australia, including from the media, can at times be inaccurate, misconstrued or just completely fictitious.

items.title: Tasmania's police minister apologises for naming Port Arthur gunman

children.content: Felix Ellis has apologised for comments he made during budget estimates about the 1996 Port Arthur massacre in which he named the gunman, with one survivor labelling his comments "hurtful" and "disrespectful".

image.altText: A bearded man seen in profile.

items.title: Boy on bail charged over stabbing of homeless man in Bendigo

children.content: Police have charged a 16-year-old boy who was on bail over the stabbing of a homeless man in Bendigo on Thursday night.

image.altText: CCTV of a street at night time, group of four walking down the road.

items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

image.altText: police albany 3

items.title: Paramedics warn of burnout and trauma as ambulance wait times exceed 'worst case scenario'

children.content: Average wait times for ambulances in Tasmania have exceeded worst-case scenario projections, despite extra resourcing, with many staff wanting to change careers, new survey results show.

image.altText: Ambulance Tasmania paramedic at rear of vehicle.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

A sleek white machine with the word "cybertongue" printed on it and small plastic pipettes next to it.

items.title: Ukraine renews attacks on St Petersburg after Putin rejects Zelenskyy  meeting

children.content: A large-scale Ukrainian drone attack targets St Petersburg, with Russia intercepting 376 drones.

image.altText: A plume of black smoke is seen over the port of St. Petersburg, Russia,

items.title: Palestinian baby killed by Israeli gunfire in occupied West Bank, health ministry says

children.content: Israeli troops shot at a car in the occupied West Bank, killing a seven-month-old baby and wounding his parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

image.altText: A man holds up a mobile phone, displaying a photo of a seven month old baby boy who is sitting on a floor, smiling.

items.title: India's 'cockroach' political movement spills onto Delhi's streets

children.content: India's largest online youth movement has taken to the streets for the first time, calling for the resignation of the education minister.

image.altText: Abhijeet Dipke, head of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), speaks to supporters of the CJP

items.title: Multiple people shot near Ohio festival, US authorities say

children.content: Police are searching for multiple suspects after at least 12 people were injured in a shooting near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio.

image.altText: Ohio police cordon off the road.

items.title: Fed-up locals get hands dirty to restore pride in Tablelands town

children.content: Atherton was once named Queensland's tidiest town, but some residents say the main street has become unsightly. Locals have formed a volunteer clean-up crew to restore the Tablelands town to its former glory.

items.title: Allegations of double billing and charging for no service at Kalgoorlie hospital

children.content: A public hospital and private medical imaging provider have been billing Medicare for some services which are not eligible for bulk billing, according to an internal memo obtained by the ABC.

items.title: Pope competes with Bad Bunny in Spain, urges leaders to reject polarisation

children.content: Pope Leo urges global leaders not to "fan the flames of polarisation" to gain popularity, warning of the artificial environment created by new technologies.

image.altText: Pope Leo stands behind protective glass in the popemobile as it moved through a crowded street escorted by security

items.title: All it took was a joke for a small town priest to meet the Pope in Vatican City

children.content: A priest and two parishioners from the Queensland town of Childers meet Pope Leo XIV in Vatican City and have a wooden cross blessed.

image.altText: The pope dressed in white robes, shakes hands with a priest dressed in black robes

items.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas

children.content: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

image.altText: Diana Ordonez runs with her arms out

items.title: Marilyn's world 'fell apart' with one post. What happened next helped her heal

children.content: Marilyn was just five when she first encountered grief. Now 18, the teenager says she's been to more funerals than birthday parties.
